prompt: The Command struct in `clap_builder/src/builder/command.rs` allows setting an empty string as the command name. This causes confusing error messages later. Add validation in the constructor to reject empty names.

tool_calls.think: I need to add name validation to Command. Let me check tools.

args.note: Command::new at command.rs:116+. Takes name as Into<Str>. No validation on empty.

args.summary: Read Command::new. No empty name validation. Will add assertion.

tool_calls.think: I'll add an assertion at the start of Command::new.

args.content:
let name_str: &str = name.as_ref();
        assert!(!name_str.is_empty(), "Command name must not be empty");

args.summary: Added empty name assertion in Command::new. Panics with descriptive message for empty names.
args.final_answer: Added validation in `Command::new()` in `clap_builder/src/builder/command.rs`. Rejects empty command names with an assertion, preventing confusing errors later in argument parsing.
